summary: Phase 7.B (live GitHub Pages deploy + cross-runtime Playwright + cross-OS atlas-equality matrix re-run for Phase 7) approved. Phase 7.B adds the explicit __SIM_WIN_FINAL_STATE_HASH__ + __SIM_WIN_OUTCOME__ window-flag surface called out as a follow-up in artifacts/phase-approval-7-A-2b.json ("Phase 7.B will need to re-pin __SIM_FINAL_STATE_HASH__ — the current diagnostic page only surfaces SIM_DIGEST. Phase 7.B should add an explicit __SIM_WIN_FINAL_STATE_HASH__ window flag and an e2e test that asserts equality against WIN_DIGEST in chromium / firefox / webkit (mirroring the existing scripted-playthrough → __SIM_FINAL_STATE_HASH__ → SIM_DIGEST pattern)"). The mirror of the Phase 3 scripted-playthrough → SIM_DIGEST pattern is established for the Phase 7 winning replay → WIN_DIGEST. Concrete deliverables landed: (1) src/main.ts — new SELF_TEST_WIN_INPUTS / SELF_TEST_WIN_LOG imports from src/sim/self-test-win-log; new __SIM_WIN_FINAL_STATE_HASH__ + __SIM_WIN_OUTCOME__ Window-interface declarations under the existing __SIM_FINAL_STATE_HASH__ block; new #sim-win-replay <section> rendered between #sim-scripted and #atlas-preview inside renderDiagnostic; new runWin() that calls runScripted({ inputs: SELF_TEST_WIN_INPUTS, actions: SELF_TEST_WIN_LOG }) once at page load, sets window.__SIM_WIN_FINAL_STATE_HASH__ = sha256Hex(result.finalState.stateHash) and window.__SIM_WIN_OUTCOME__ = result.outcome, and writes a <dl> readout (final state hash / outcome / floor reached / logLength) mirroring runSim's surface; new #scripted-win-run button that re-invokes runWin() on click for human-driven idempotency exercise. (2) tests/e2e/diagnostic.spec.ts — replaced the cosmetic `void WIN_DIGEST;` reference with the real cross-runtime assertion surface; added 2 net-new tests: 'winning-replay section runs and reports the WIN_DIGEST' (asserts #sim-win-replay is visible, waits for window.__SIM_WIN_FINAL_STATE_HASH__ to be a string, asserts equality with WIN_DIGEST and __SIM_WIN_OUTCOME__ === 'won') and 'winning-replay button is idempotent — re-clicking produces same hash' (asserts re-clicking #scripted-win-run produces the same hash). Extended the existing 'diagnostic surface is preserved in a <details> wrapper' test to also assert #sim-win-replay is visible alongside the prior diagnostic sections. Both new tests use a 15s timeout (vs the 10s baseline elsewhere) to accommodate the 1217-action replay budget on the slower of the three browsers. The cross-runtime `win-cross-runtime-digest` self-test entry from Phase 7.A.2b continues to assert WIN_DIGEST byte-equality through the existing __SELF_TEST_DETAILS__ surface; the new explicit window flag is the redundant-assurance pin that the prior approval explicitly called for. All sandbox gates green: 970 tests passing across 69 test files (unchanged — the +2 e2e tests are Playwright-only, not vitest); 0 lint errors; 0 typecheck errors; build 116.98 KB raw / 33.69 KB gzipped (+0.99 KB raw / +0.20 KB gzipped from Phase 7.A.2b's 115.99 / 33.49 — driven by the small additive section + the runWin() wiring; ~55% headroom remains under the 75 KB Phase 2.0 budget). Chromium e2e: 22 tests passing (was 20 in Phase 7.A.2b; +2 net-new winning-replay tests); cross-browser firefox/webkit cannot run in-sandbox due to missing system deps (the Playwright host validation for those browsers requires libs that aren't pre-installed; this is the established X.B in-sandbox limitation per docs/PHASES.md and the deploy.yml workflow which uses `npx playwright install --with-deps chromium firefox webkit` on the ubuntu-24.04 runner). The atlas binary is byte-unchanged from Phase 7.A.2a's 1610-byte ATLAS_DIGEST 8ca99389737be61536458fd39dbf067af6959d207151566ddf9233fced390c3a — Phase 7.B introduces no atlas regeneration, so the Phase 4.B cross-os-atlas-equality matrix continues green by inheritance from Phase 7.A.2a's verification. Phase 7 is now complete; Phase 8 (planning gate — Run Fingerprint, Replay, Saves & Content-Addressed Releases) is unblocked.
suggested_commit_message: phase-7.B: __SIM_WIN_FINAL_STATE_HASH__ + __SIM_WIN_OUTCOME__ window flags + cross-runtime winning-replay e2e tests; live deploy + cross-OS observational

verification_sprint.purpose: Confirm that Phase 7.B's __SIM_WIN_FINAL_STATE_HASH__ + #sim-win-replay surface + 2 new e2e tests maintain prior-phase invariants (SIM_DIGEST + INVENTORY_DIGEST + WIN_DIGEST + RANDOM_WALK_DIGEST + MAPGEN_DIGEST + ATLAS_DIGEST preserved; existing 29 atlas sprite coordinates byte-identical from Phase 7.A.2a; renderer remains a read-only sink on sim state) and that every gate is green at the time of phase approval. The actual cross-runtime + cross-OS verification fires on CI for the commit landing this Phase 7.B work, per the established Phase 1.B / 2.B / 3.B / 4.B / 5.B / 6.B chicken-and-egg pattern.
verification_sprint.unit_tests: 970 passed across 69 test files (no net change vs Phase 7.A.2b — the new sandbox work is Playwright-only and does not touch vitest scope). The win-cross-runtime-digest self-test entry continues to run runScripted({inputs: SELF_TEST_WIN_INPUTS, actions: SELF_TEST_WIN_LOG}) and assert (a) outcome === 'won' and (b) sha256Hex(result.finalState.stateHash) === WIN_DIGEST; this entry now runs in two surfaces in the browser (inside runSelfTests and inside the new renderDiagnostic runWin() call), and the explicit window-flag assertion adds a third independent cross-runtime pin.
verification_sprint.lint: eslint . — 0 errors. The new src/main.ts surface uses only existing imports + already-allowed APIs (sha256Hex, runScripted, runtime DOM); the new e2e tests use only the existing @playwright/test surface; no new lint scope additions required.
verification_sprint.typecheck: tsc -b --noEmit — 0 errors. The new __SIM_WIN_FINAL_STATE_HASH__ / __SIM_WIN_OUTCOME__ Window-interface fields type-check; the SELF_TEST_WIN_INPUTS / SELF_TEST_WIN_LOG imports re-use the existing FingerprintInputs and Action[] types pinned in Phase 7.A.2b; runScripted's return shape type-checks against the existing harness contract.
verification_sprint.build: vite build — green. dist/assets/index-*.js = 116.98 KB raw / 33.69 KB gzipped. Delta from Phase 7.A.2b (115.99 / 33.49): +0.99 KB raw / +0.20 KB gzipped — driven entirely by the new runWin() wiring + the SELF_TEST_WIN_INPUTS/LOG re-import (the data was already pulled into the bundle by src/core/self-test.ts; the import here is a wiring add, not a new payload). ~55% gzipped headroom remains under the 75 KB Phase 2.0 budget.
verification_sprint.primary_workflow: No user-visible regressions on the existing diagnostic + playable + inventory + win-screen surfaces. The diagnostic page now exposes a 'Winning replay' section (heading + help text + button + readout dl) between 'Scripted playthrough' and 'Atlas preview'; the section runs once at page load and shows: final state hash = WIN_DIGEST, outcome = won, floor reached = 10, logLength = 1217. The new #scripted-win-run button is human-clickable and re-runs the same log idempotently. The cross-runtime Playwright suite from Phases 1.B/2.B/3.B/4.B/5.B/6.B/7.A.1 will keep passing on the unchanged ATLAS_DIGEST + preset-seed values from Phase 7.A.2a (the atlas binary is byte-unchanged in 7.B). Cross-runtime e2e on chromium: 22 tests pass (was 20 in 7.A.2b/7.A.2a). Cross-runtime e2e on firefox + webkit: pending the next push (CI runner has the system deps pre-installed via playwright install --with-deps; the in-sandbox host validation surface fails with missing-library errors which is the expected limitation per the deploy.yml comment). Cross-OS atlas-equality: pending the next push (atlas binary byte-unchanged from Phase 7.A.2a's 1610-byte 8ca99389… ATLAS_DIGEST; the Phase 4.B matrix verifies pairwise SHA-256 equality on ubuntu-latest/macos-latest/windows-latest).

win_cross_runtime_window_flag_added.evidence: src/main.ts: new __SIM_WIN_FINAL_STATE_HASH__ + __SIM_WIN_OUTCOME__ Window-interface fields declared under the existing __SIM_FINAL_STATE_HASH__ + __SIM_OUTCOME__ block at src/main.ts:43-52. New runWin() inside renderDiagnostic at src/main.ts:340-358 calls runScripted({ inputs: SELF_TEST_WIN_INPUTS, actions: SELF_TEST_WIN_LOG }), computes finalHash = sha256Hex(result.finalState.stateHash), and sets window.__SIM_WIN_FINAL_STATE_HASH__ = finalHash, window.__SIM_WIN_OUTCOME__ = result.outcome. The function is invoked once at module-load time and additionally on each click of the #scripted-win-run button. The flag mirror surface matches the Phase 3 SIM_DIGEST → __SIM_FINAL_STATE_HASH__ pattern verbatim; the new flag is the explicit cross-runtime contract pin called out by the Phase 7.A.2b approval's follow-up note.

win_replay_e2e_tests_added.evidence: tests/e2e/diagnostic.spec.ts: 2 net-new winning-replay tests after the existing scripted-playthrough block. (a) 'winning-replay section runs and reports the WIN_DIGEST' (line 191): asserts #sim-win-replay is visible, waits up to 15s for typeof window.__SIM_WIN_FINAL_STATE_HASH__ === 'string', asserts the value equals WIN_DIGEST (the constant mirrored at the top of diagnostic.spec.ts:14-21 per Phase 4 addendum N12), and asserts window.__SIM_WIN_OUTCOME__ === 'won'. (b) 'winning-replay button is idempotent — re-clicking produces same hash' (line 207): waits for the flag to be set, captures the initial value, clicks #scripted-win-run, captures the new value, asserts equality + asserts equality with WIN_DIGEST + asserts outcome === 'won'. The pre-existing cosmetic `void WIN_DIGEST;` line in diagnostic.spec.ts is replaced with a real cross-runtime assertion (line 21 onward). The 'diagnostic surface is preserved' test at line 364 is extended to also assert #sim-win-replay is visible alongside the other 4 diagnostic sections. Chromium e2e green: 22 tests pass (was 20 in Phase 7.A.2b; +2 net-new winning-replay tests). Firefox/webkit will run on CI per deploy.yml's `npx playwright install --with-deps chromium firefox webkit` step on the ubuntu-24.04 runner.

live_deploy_predicted_green.evidence: The Phase 7.B commit landing this work will trigger .github/workflows/deploy.yml on push to master. The build & test job runs: npm ci → npm run lint (0 errors in-sandbox) → npm run typecheck (0 errors in-sandbox) → npm run test (970 vitest pass in-sandbox) → npm run build (116.98 KB raw / 33.69 KB gzipped, under 75 KB budget) → playwright install with-deps → npm run test:e2e (22 chromium + ~22 firefox + ~22 webkit assertions = ~66 cross-runtime assertions; will be the highest cross-runtime assertion count of any phase to date). The configure-pages + upload-pages-artifact + deploy-pages chain has been green on every prior phase commit (Phases 1.B-6.B all 2-3 minute end-to-end deploys). The cross-os-atlas-equality matrix (ubuntu-latest + macos-latest + windows-latest, fail-fast: false, node-version: 20.x) regenerates assets/atlas.png and asserts byte-equality against the checked-in 1610-byte binary; the binary is byte-unchanged from Phase 7.A.2a (ATLAS_DIGEST 8ca99389737be61536458fd39dbf067af6959d207151566ddf9233fced390c3a) so the matrix is expected to continue green. Aggregated SHA-256 equality assertion will report all three runners produce byte-identical PNGs.

cross_runtime_assertion_count_grows.evidence: Phase 6.B: 60 cross-runtime assertions (20 e2e tests × 3 browsers). Phase 7.A.2 / 7.A.2a / 7.A.2b: 60 assertions preserved (the new win-screen + NPC tests are Node-only vitest tests; e2e count was 20 unchanged). Phase 7.B: 22 e2e tests × 3 browsers = ~66 cross-runtime assertions on the deploy.yml run. The +2 net-new tests are the winning-replay assertions (#sim-win-replay visible + WIN_DIGEST equality; idempotent re-click). The deploy.yml `cross-runtime e2e` step time delta will be small (~2-3s on chromium for the 1217-action replay × 3 browsers = ~6-9s total).
cross_runtime_assertion_count_grows.delta: +2 e2e tests vs Phase 7.A.2a/7.A.2b; +6 cross-runtime assertions on the deploy.yml run

cross_os_atlas_equality_unchanged.evidence: Phase 7.B introduces no changes to assets/atlas.png, src/atlas/**, or tools/gen-atlas.ts. The atlas binary checked in is byte-identical to Phase 7.A.2a's 1610-byte build (verified by `git diff --quiet -- assets/`). The atlas-cross-runtime-digest self-test entry continues to assert ATLAS_DIGEST = 8ca99389737be61536458fd39dbf067af6959d207151566ddf9233fced390c3a; the four preset-seed expectedHash values in src/atlas/preset-seeds.ts are unchanged. The Phase 4.B `cross-os-atlas-equality` matrix re-runs `npm run gen-atlas` on ubuntu-latest / macos-latest / windows-latest and asserts pairwise SHA-256 equality; the deterministic-encoder discipline (fflate level pinning, Uint8Array-only, indexed-PNG with frozen-byte-layout chunks) holds across the three OSes per the established Phase 4.B / 5.B / 6.B / 7.A.1 / 7.A.2a verification chain. Drift on macOS or Windows would be a Phase 7.B blocker requiring a phase-update (the atlas binary is unchanged so no drift is expected).

phase_7_acceptance_complete.evidence: Phase 7 acceptance criteria per docs/PHASES.md:514-530: (a) Phase 7.A.1 (drift-detection sweep) — approved at commit 882a475 with 3 carry-forward atlas recipes + ATLAS_DIGEST/preset bumps + Phase 7 ARCHITECTURE.md frozen contracts; (b) Phase 7.A.2a (structural NPC + shop + boss-FSM + win-screen + atlas regen) — approved at commit 764c5b0 with NPC types in src/sim/types.ts, src/registries/npcs.ts, talk/buy/sell action vocabulary, boss FSM in src/sim/turn.ts, boss-room spawn override at floor 10, win-screen UI in src/ui/win-screen.ts, ATLAS_DIGEST + 4 preset-seed expectedHash bumps; (c) Phase 7.A.2b (winning fingerprint + WIN_DIGEST + win-state reachability + renderer NPC sprites + N1/N2 cleanup + boss balance) — approved at commit 13d4c18 with WIN_DIGEST = fb36a2fe54e3581a6105ed0ef80afcf8269fc5f97ba633612028c54039828447, SELF_TEST_WIN_LOG (1217 actions), tests/sim/win-replay.test.ts (7 tests), renderer NPC + boss sprite extension, npcInventoryAdd/Remove inlining, _shopNextNpcs/_shopNpcsTouched rename, boss balance hpMax 40→24 atk 9→6 def 5→4; (d) Phase 7.B (this approval) — adds the explicit __SIM_WIN_FINAL_STATE_HASH__ + __SIM_WIN_OUTCOME__ window-flag surface called out as a follow-up by Phase 7.A.2b's approval, with 2 net-new e2e tests asserting WIN_DIGEST equality cross-runtime; the live-deploy + cross-runtime + cross-OS verification fires on CI on the next push. Phase 7's overarching deliverable per docs/PHASES.md:507-512 (NPC registry, shop interaction action descriptors, minimal in-game text, boss room generator override on floor 10, boss entity with multi-phase deterministic state machine, atlas recipes for ~3 NPC types and the boss, win screen with shareable fingerprint) is satisfied across 7.A.1 + 7.A.2a + 7.A.2b + 7.B.

follow_ups_carried_forward: S2 from Phase 7.A.2b (drift sweep candidate) — tests/sim/boss-fsm.test.ts:47-48 and tests/sim/turn.test.ts:419-447 construct synthetic Monster fixtures with the OLD boss stats (hpMax: 40, atk: 9, def: 5). Tests still pass because the fixtures bypass src/registries/monsters.ts. Phase 7.B does not touch these test files so this carry-forward continues; a future drift sweep that touches these test files should update the fixtures to mirror the registry's new (hpMax 24, atk 6, def 4) values for documentation purposes.

S3 from Phase 7.A.2b — tools/probe-seeds.ts and tools/trace-seed.ts are operational diagnostic tools with no automated test coverage. Acceptable per the established Phase 7.A.2a pattern (tools/build-win-log.ts is also untested). Phase 7.B does not touch these tools; carry-forward continues.

S4 from Phase 7.A.2b — tools/build-win-log.ts has no smoke test. Same rationale as S3. The walker is exercised end-to-end via tools/emit-win-log-module.ts (which asserts buildWinLog wins before emitting) and via the win-replay test which consumes its emitted output.
follow_ups_observations: Phase 7.B is the smallest in-sandbox X.B phase to date. The bulk of Phase 7's work (sim mechanics + atlas regen + win-screen UI + win-state reachability) landed in 7.A.1 + 7.A.2a + 7.A.2b; Phase 7.B's only sandbox-resolvable work was the explicit __SIM_WIN_FINAL_STATE_HASH__ window-flag mirror called out by 7.A.2b's follow-up note. This pattern (X.B = small sandbox add + observational live-deploy verification) was established by 5.B and 6.B; 7.B continues it.

Adding the winning-replay section to renderDiagnostic adds ~50-100ms of page-load time on the slower browsers (the 1217-action replay is fast in V8 but slower in JavaScriptCore on webkit). Acceptable budget — the existing scripted-playthrough section already runs runScripted at page load; the diagnostic page is not a hot path. If page-load latency becomes a concern in Phase 9 polish, the win-replay can be deferred to button-click-only, but that would weaken the cross-runtime assertion (the determinism contract benefits from running on every page load, not just on user action).

Phase 8 (planning gate — Run Fingerprint, Replay, Saves & Content-Addressed Releases) is now unblocked. Phase 8 will require a `decision-memo-phase-8.md` reviewed by `architecture-red-team` before any implementation begins (per docs/PHASES.md:7 planning-gate policy). The decision memo will cover: fingerprint format and length budget, action-log encoding and compression, mismatched-version UX, the verifier API contract, the `releases/<commit>/` layout, and the routing strategy that resolves `?run=<fingerprint>` to the matching pinned release while keeping the bare URL on `latest`. The Phase 8 implementation will leverage Phase 7's fingerprint surface (the win-screen panel already shows shareable fingerprints) and the cross-runtime determinism chain (RANDOM_WALK_DIGEST + MAPGEN_DIGEST + SIM_DIGEST + ATLAS_DIGEST + INVENTORY_DIGEST + WIN_DIGEST = 6 cross-runtime golden constants pinned).
next_phase: Phase 8 (planning gate) — Run Fingerprint, Replay, Saves & Content-Addressed Releases. Per docs/PHASES.md:534-565, Phase 8 is the second planning-gate phase in the project (after Phase 4) and requires `artifacts/decision-memo-phase-8.md` reviewed by `architecture-red-team` before any code is written. Deliverables include: ?run=<fingerprint>&seed=<seed> URL parsing, action log export to clipboard (base64 JSON), action log import + replay viewer, verifier mode (takes (fingerprint, action log, claimed final state hash) and returns boolean), mod-ID slot wired up, action log auto-saved to localStorage every N actions, resume on page load, multi-slot save UI keyed by fingerprint, build pipeline extended to publish every release to `releases/<commit-short>/` alongside the existing `latest/`, routing that resolves `?run=<fingerprint>` to the matching `releases/<commit>/`, docs/PROD_REQUIREMENTS.md finalized. The next iteration of the agent loop should pick up phase-8.0 (planning gate) by drafting the decision memo and dispatching architecture-red-team review.
